resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def findUnsortedSubarray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        sorted_nums = sorted(nums)
        start, end = 0, len(nums) - 1
        while start < len(nums) and nums[start] == sorted_nums[start]:
            start += 1
        while end >= 0 and nums[end] == sorted_nums[end]:
            end -= 1
        return end - start + 1 if start <= end else 0
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution works by first sorting the input array. Then, it finds the start and end indices of the unsorted subarray by comparing the sorted array with the original array. The time complexity is dominated by the sorting operation, which is O(n log n). The space complexity is O(n) due to the sorting operation.
